preamble: The transformation from a voiced postalveolar affricate ([d͡ʒ]) to a voiceless alveolar fricative ([s]) involves a series of phonetic changes including devoicing, affricate reduction (spirantization), and fronting of the place of articulation. This shift is attested in specific morpho-phonological contexts.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing, Affricate Reduction (Spirantization), Fronting (Postalveolar to Alveolar)

examples.note: Morpho-phonological devoicing of the plural suffix /d͡ʒa/ to /sa/ when following a plosive, with the preceding plosive being deleted. For example, /ra.mut/ 'root' + /d͡ʒa/ (plural suffix) becomes /ra.mu.sa/ 'root-pl'.

sources: Klamer, M. (2019). The Flores-Lembata languages in eastern Indonesia. LOT Publications.
